items.title: New scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means

children.content: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

gallery.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

thumbnail.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: 'Into the hands of Trump': Dan Duggan's bid to avoid extradition fails

children.content: Former United States Marine Corps pilot Dan Duggan’s Federal Court bid to avoid extradition from Australia is dismissed.

image.altText: Former US military pilot Daniel Edmund Duggan seen as a pilot in Tasmania

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: 'Plate to paddock': How a festival's waste became a farmer's treasure

children.content: Tonnes of organic waste from the National Folk Festival are being turned into fertiliser on a farm outside Canberra in a circular economy project delivering more benefits than just waste reduction.

gallery.altText: A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.
A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.
A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.

A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.
A man ina green shirt stands on his farm.

items.title: 'Your courage does not end': Prince Harry addresses room of veterans in Canberra

children.content: The Duke of Sussex flew solo to the nation's capital and addressed an Invictus Australia event at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: Prince Harry attends an Invictus Australia reception at the Aust

items.title: Pressure increases on CIT CEO to stand down

children.content: An independent MLA is calling for the head of CIT to stand down, claiming statutory declarations show she "clearly misled" the board prior to her appointment, despite knowing about misconduct findings.

image.altText: Man with a beard wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Two men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Irma Palasics

children.content: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

gallery.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.
A man stands outside a court house speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Australia enters drone warfare era with Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark

children.content: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

items.title: The PM is not about to pick a fight over a gas tax

children.content: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

image.altText: A close up of Albanese, who wears a neutral expression

items.title: Outcry over 'disturbing' video of off-duty police officer pinning 13yo girl on train

children.content: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

image.altText: A young girl holds her head and winces with her face being blurred.

items.title: Harry and Meghan seek duty and dollars on tour

children.content: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?

gallery.altText: Harry and Meghan pose with a group of surf life savers inside a surf club.

thumbnail.altText: Harry and Meghan pose with a group of surf life savers inside a surf club.

items.title: As Trump spruiks Lebanon ceasefire, some want war to continue

children.content: Donald Trump sold a ceasefire in Lebanon as a step towards peace. But that promise hinges on a condition that Lebanon may not be able to deliver.

image.altText: Donald Trump in a suit and tie speaking outside the Oval Office, holding his hand out in front of him.

items.title: An executive at FIFA's new betting partner settled an insider-trading lawsuit

children.content: The backstory of the prediction market industry's newest entrant has done little to calm concerns as it partners with several giants of sports broadcasting.

image.altText: Collage graphic featuring picture of man wearing business suit, football players, share chart and cryptocurrency.

items.title: National 'Walk for Truth' asks PM to deliver on long-awaited promise

children.content: Indigenous leader Travis Lovett sets off on a trek to Canberra, calling for action from the federal government to support a national truth-telling process for First Nations people.

gallery.altText: Commissioner Travis Lovett raising a message stick on the steps of Parliament House with a crowd of supporters behind him.

thumbnail.altText: Commissioner Travis Lovett raising a message stick on the steps of Parliament House with a crowd of supporters behind him.

items.title: Desperate for social media ban to work, but parents say it’s 'impossible'

children.content: Tech companies are coming under fire as Australian parents increasingly voice concerns that some online platforms are failing to keep kids under 16 off their platforms.

image.altText: close up of social media apps on iphone screen including facebook, messenger, tiktok, instagram, threads and whatsapp

items.title: Not all city suburbs have NBN access and it isn't by accident. Here's why

children.content: Despite popular belief, not all Australians are guaranteed access to the NBN — and as many consumers have discovered, alternative networks do not always provide quality service.

image.altText: A graphic showing a person's hands on a laptop with a Wifi failure symbol superimposed over the top.

avatar.alt: An Australian-Egyptian woman with dark brown shoulder-length curly hair smiling at camera, in a green patterned top.

items.title: Do acupuncture and herbs actually help with menopause symptoms?

children.content: Complementary therapies may help some menopausal symptoms, but more high-quality research is needed to understand how effective and safe these treatments truly are.

gallery.altText: A woman inserts needles into a hand.

thumbnail.altText: A woman inserts needles into a hand.

items.title: Old is new again: Gen alpha takes to old tech with fresh eyes

children.content: With the constant evolution of the digital world, community history is rapidly disappearing into memories and museums. But a historical society on WA's south coast is delighting today's kids by showing them the technology older Australians grew up with.

image.altText: A young girl looks into the rear of a bellows camera

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: The three members of Nirvana pose for a photo.

items.title: The Aussie farmer making waves in Kenya

children.content: After moving to Kenya 15 years ago Aussie farmer Stuart Barden's dream has become reality. The 55-year-old is helping change the face of the country's cropping industry.

gallery.altText: Australian farmer Stuart Barden standing on top of a former hyena's den in Kenya

thumbnail.altText: Australian farmer Stuart Barden standing on top of a former hyena's den in Kenya

items.title: Patients with little-known neurological condition wait years for diagnosis

children.content: People with functional neurological disorder say they experience misdiagnosis and struggles within the medical system.

image.altText: A woman stands smiling and holding her arms out in front of large red rocks.

items.title: Toddler's death at Perth hospital was 'probably preventable'

children.content: The death of a toddler at one of Perth's major hospitals was "probably preventable" and the failure to perform a blood test on him was "more than a missed opportunity", a coroner finds.

image.altText: A boy dressed in winter clothes sitting on a black couch

items.title: LIV Golf facing fight to 'keep going', CEO admits in deleted interview

children.content: The future of LIV Golf remains in doubt as chief executive Scott O'Neil admits in an interview that the league is "funded through the season" but work is required beyond that "to keep us going".

image.altText: Scott O'Neil walks on the first tee box in front of LIV Golf signage

items.title: Flood-damaged roads cause 'catastrophic' impacts for NT businesses

children.content: Workers in road-reliant industries are calling on the federal government to provide urgent funding to repair the NT's road network, which was severely damaged by floods.

image.altText: A drone shot shows a damaged dirt road alongside a brown river, with a massive pothole missing from one section.

items.title: Inside the legal feud between Gina Rinehart and Angela Bennett

children.content: Their fathers were school buddies turned mining pioneers, and in another life they could have been friends — but Gina Rinehart and Angela Bennett came to be opponents in one of the most high-stakes legal battles Australia has ever seen.

gallery.altText: A background image of a mine site, with pictures of four mining figures from left to right.

thumbnail.altText: A background image of a mine site, with pictures of four mining figures from left to right.

items.title: How lawyers are using AI, and financial planners aren't far behind

children.content: Businesses say they are using AI to speed up service delivery and improve the customer experience, but economists warn this may be the "calm before the storm".

image.altText: A graphic image of a brain on a blue background.

items.title: Fremantle Prison break remembered 150 years on as 'ultimate story' of hope and freedom

children.content: The story of how a Fenian captain from Massachusetts defied the odds and rescued six Irish political convicts from a prison on the very edge of the world.

items.title: World Cup fans to be charged $200 for 15 minute train trip

children.content: A 15-minute round-trip train fare from New York to New Jersey's MetLife Stadium will set World Cup football fans back more than $200 as officials struggle to cover rising costs.

image.altText: Football fans kick a ball around in the carpark outside MetLife Stadium

items.title: Amid misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta is set to break new ground

children.content: Amid a storm of misogynistic criticism, Marie-Louise Eta will become the first woman to take charge of a men's football team in one of Europe's top five leagues. But she just wants everyone to focus on the football.

items.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains changes

items.title: Producer backs pitch for major film studio in Port Kembla

children.content: As the New South Wales government searches for a site for a second Sydney film studio, one of Australia's most prominent producers suggests a regional location is the ideal fit.
